task_completion.description: Launch the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Liverpool Cancer iChart app interface is visible on the screen.
The cancer drug 'Crizotinib' is selected or checked in the cancer drugs list or search interface.
The co-medication 'Acenocoumarol' is selected or checked in the co-medications list.

task_completion.description: Select the second co-medication Fluconazole concurrently
task_completion.visual_evidence: The co-medication 'Fluconazole' is selected or checked in the co-medications list alongside Acenocoumarol, demonstrating a multi-select query.

task_completion.description: Navigate to and remain on the combined results screen showing both interactions
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final screen is the Results/Interactions screen displaying 'Crizotinib' interacting with BOTH 'Acenocoumarol' and 'Fluconazole' simultaneously, showing their respective interaction ratings (traffic light colors).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Cancer iChart GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the native Android app interface for Cancer iChart being used, rather than terminal commands, web browsers, or text editors.

integrity.description: The results screen is generated by the app, not manually typed
integrity.visual_evidence: The interaction results appear within the app's standard UI elements (e.g., traffic light icons, specific app formatting) and not in a notepad or terminal output.

integrity.description: Both co-medications were queried in a single combined search
integrity.visual_evidence: The final results screen shows both drugs listed together in a single view, rather than two separate sequential searches.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Cancer iChart app (com.liverpooluni.ichartoncology) is an offline drug interaction checker that uses a Traffic Light system (red/amber/yellow/green) to indicate interaction severity. Crizotinib is the target cancer drug. Acenocoumarol and Fluconazole are the target co-medications. The task specifically requires a multi-select workflow where BOTH co-medications are selected before viewing the results, so the final screen must display the interaction ratings for Crizotinib against both Acenocoumarol and Fluconazole concurrently.
